content.json:
# JA4 / JA4S Threat-Hunting Workbook

**What this is.** Every HTTPS connection carries a **JA4** fingerprint of the *software* that made it (the browser, app, script, or malware) and a **JA4S** fingerprint of the *server* that answered. Malware and hacking tools have distinctive fingerprints, and unusual fingerprints stand out. This workbook surfaces the most interesting TLS fingerprints in your estate and **explains why each one matters** so you can act without being a JA4 expert.

## How to triage (start here)
1. Open **Section = Top Leads** (the cross-engine priority queue). Work the **red / highest-Score** rows first.
2. Read the **Why / Reasons** column - it states in plain language why the row is interesting.
3. Confirm the **process** and **destination** (what program is talking, and to where). A trusted program to a trusted site is usually benign; an unknown/LOLBIN program to a raw IP or odd domain is not.
4. If it looks malicious: isolate the device in Defender, raise/update the incident, and pivot using the device + IP shown.
5. Then sweep the other sections for corroboration (a device that appears in **multiple** sections is high-confidence).

## Reading the results
- **Verdict / Severity**: **Critical** > **High** > **Medium** > **Low**. Critical = act now; Low = review when capacity allows.
- **Red heatmap** columns (Score / SuspicionScore / MatchFidelity / BeaconScore / Devices): **darker red = more urgent**.
- **Green heatmap** (Rarity): **darker green = rarer** (1.0 = a near-unique fingerprint; rare is more interesting).
- **JA4 / JA4S** are the raw fingerprints (`t13d...` / `t12...`); you do not need to decode them - the **Why** column already has.

## Sections at a glance
- **Top Leads** - the single ranked queue across all engines. *Start here.*
- **Suspicious triage** - every rare fingerprint scored for maliciousness (process / cert / structure).
- **Incident match** - fingerprints tied to your existing incidents, by closeness.
- **Beaconing** - regular low-jitter call-homes (classic C2 shape).
- **New & cipher-cycling** - brand-new fingerprints and cipher-rotating evasion.
- **C2 tradecraft** - process<->fingerprint contradictions and Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter shapes.
- **Endpoint corroboration** - rare JA4 fused with downloads, ASR/alerts, and process lineage.
- **Identity & cloud** - rare JA4 fused with risky sign-ins, cloud exfil, and phishing.
- **Destination anomalies** - trusted-site abuse, hidden-SNI/ECH, and shadow-IT.
- **Inventory & hygiene** - deprecated TLS, impossible fingerprints, cross-OS anomalies.
- **Process baseline** - the known-good (process -> fingerprint) catalog (reference).
- **Known-bad (opt-in)** - exact known-malware fingerprint matches (toggle on).

Pick a **Lookback** and **Min rarity** below, then a **Section** (only the selected one runs, so it stays fast). Core detections use Microsoft E5 / Defender-native data only - no external feeds (the opt-in Known-bad section uses public FoxIO reference data).

content.json: **How to use this workbook.** Start at **Section 1 - Top Leads**. **Red pills = act now**, amber = review, question/blue = informational. Hover any **Client JA4 / Server JA4S / TLS library** column for a plain-language explanation. Every section has a **Next step** telling a Tier-1 analyst exactly what to do. Toggle **Known-bad lookup = On** (above) to also run exact known-malware fingerprint matches.

## Top Prioritized Leads - what's interesting and why
The highest-signal items across all engines, ranked. **Why** explains each: a suspicious fingerprint, a fingerprint tied to an incident's device/IP, or a periodic call-home. Work the top (red) rows first.

**Next step:** for each red row, read **Why**, confirm the **process + destination**, pivot to the device timeline in Defender; isolate the device and raise an incident if it looks malicious.

tooltipFormat.tooltip: 0-100: the strongest engine score for this lead (suspicion, beaconing or incident-fidelity). Higher = more urgent. Work red rows first.

TLS fingerprint of the SERVER that answered. JA4 + JA4S together identify a specific application or malware family.
The cipher-hash part of the JA4 (chars 11-22) - a stable identifier of the TLS LIBRARY (Chromium / Firefox / Python / Go / WinINET). A library that does not match the process is suspicious.
Coefficient of variation (stdev/mean) of the gaps between connections. Below ~0.2 = very regular = C2-like; above ~1 = irregular = benign.
Average minutes between call-homes to this destination.
How closely the fingerprint ties to the incident: flow-exact (same flow) > host+time > host-any > shared-IP.
The exact process-vs-TLS-library contradiction detected (e.g. a browser library from a non-browser process).
Span from first-seen to last-seen. A fingerprint active for many days across many hosts is usually benign.
Minutes between the JA4 callout and the corroborating event (sign-in / cloud upload). Smaller = stronger correlation.
Minutes between the phishing email delivery and the rare callout. Under ~10 = likely an automated click-to-implant callback.
Time between this fingerprints connection and the incident alert. Smaller = a tighter match.
How many different destination IPs this fingerprint reached.
Total connections observed for this fingerprint pair.
Hours between the first and last beacon in this series.

### Process vs fingerprint contradictions
A program whose TLS **library** doesn't match the program itself (a browser fingerprint from a non-browser, or any TLS from a script-host LOLBIN) - uTLS parroting, DLL injection, or PUP/adware. High-signal even when the fingerprint isn't rare.

content.json:
### Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter TLS shape
TLS 1.2 + no-ALPN from a LOLBIN to a non-Microsoft destination, or an exact Cobalt Strike fingerprint.

content.query:
// Workbook: C2 TLS-SHAPE (Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter). Malleable-C2 cannot change the WinINET/WinHTTP
// ClientHello: TLS 1.2 + no-ALPN + a LOLBIN/loader process to a public, non-Microsoft dest. Exact CS tells
// (client c-section 16bbda4055b2 / server JA4S t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92) = CRITICAL on any process.
let lookback = {Lookback};
let csJa4s = "t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92";
let csCsec = "16bbda4055b2";
let suspProcs = dynamic(["rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","msbuild.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","regsvcs.exe","regasm.exe"]);
let msSni = dynamic([".microsoft.com",".windows.com",".windowsupdate.com",".office.com",".office365.com",".sharepoint.com",".onedrive.com",".microsoftonline.com",".azure.com",".azureedge.net",".msedge.net",".msftconnecttest.com",".windows.net"]);
let ssl =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), ja4s_ = tostring(AF.ja4s), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where strlen(ja4_) >= 36
    | extend tlsVer = substring(ja4_, 1, 2), alpn = substring(ja4_, 8, 2), csec = substring(ja4_, 24, 12)
    | extend isPublic = (isnotempty(RemoteIP) and ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) == false)
    | where isPublic and ((tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00") or ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
    | summarize Conns = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ja4_, ja4s_, tlsVer, alpn, csec, sni;
let procs =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" and isnotempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
    | summarize ProcFolder = take_any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, Proc = tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName);
ssl
| join hint.shufflekey=DeviceId kind=inner procs on DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| extend exactCs   = (ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
| extend shape     = (tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00")
| extend suspProc  = (Proc in~ (suspProcs))
| extend isUserPath = (ProcFolder has "appdata" or ProcFolder hasprefix "c:\\users\\" or ProcFolder has "\\temp\\")
| extend isMsDest  = (isnotempty(sni) and sni has_any (msSni))
| where exactCs or (shape and suspProc and not(isMsDest))
| extend Why = strcat(iff(csec == csCsec, "CS client c-section; ", ""), iff(ja4s_ == csJa4s, "CS server JA4S; ", ""),
                      iff(shape, "TLS1.2+no-ALPN; ", ""), iff(suspProc, strcat("proc ", Proc, "; "), ""), iff(isUserPath, "user/temp path; ", ""))
| summarize Conns = sum(Conns), Devices = dcount(DeviceId, 4), Procs = make_set(Proc, 5), Dests = make_set(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), 6),
            AnyExactCs = max(exactCs), AnyUserPath = max(isUserPath), Why = take_any(Why), FirstSeen = min(FirstSeen), LastSeen = max(LastSeen)
        by ja4_, ja4s_
| extend Verdict = iff(AnyExactCs, "CRITICAL - Cobalt Strike fingerprint", "HIGH - C2 TLS shape + suspicious process")
| project Verdict, ja4_, ja4s_, Why, Procs, Devices, Conns, AnyUserPath, Dests, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| sort by Verdict asc, Devices asc, Conns asc
| take 200   // display cap: highest-severity first; detection logic above is uncapped

## Summary
At-a-glance counts. **Rare pairs** and **self-signed certs** are where to start. Use the **Section** selector above to dive in.

## Suspicious rare JA4 - triage
Every rare JA4 x JA4S pair scored for maliciousness. **Verdict / Reasons** say *why* (LOLBIN process, self-signed/self-CA cert, no-SNI, no-extensions, abnormal cipher count...). **Procs** is the process behind the fingerprint (recovered via ConnectionSuccess). Verdict tops out at **Critical**.

**Next step:** work **Critical/High** first; verify the **Procs** value is a program you expect on that host, check the **SampleSNIs/Issuers** destination, then review the device timeline. Unknown process + self-signed cert to a public IP = escalate.

tooltipFormat.tooltip: 0-100: weighted malice signals (LOLBIN +35, legacy-TLS +25, self-signed-public +30, no-extensions +20, SNI=IP +15, few/many-ciphers, no-SNI/no-ALPN, user-path) minus benign discounts. >=80 Critical, >=50 High, >=30 Medium.

## Incident-match fidelity
Which rare fingerprints are tied to your incidents and how tightly. **MatchTier** = flow-exact > host+time > host-any > shared-IP; **MatchFidelity** decays by time-gap and is weighted by rarity and incident severity. **DrivingIncident** is the incident it matched.

**Next step:** open the **DrivingIncident**; the fingerprint is extra evidence for that case. A **flow-exact** match at high fidelity means this exact TLS flow is part of the incident - add it to the investigation and sweep other hosts for the same JA4.

content.json:
## Beaconing call-homes
Regular, low-jitter TLS to one destination = classic C2 shape. A **rare** JA4 to a **no-SNI / raw-IP** destination with low **JitterCV** is the top lead. Benign telemetry (Microsoft, AV, browsers) also beacons - triage by **Dest**.

**Next step:** judge the **Dest** - a known vendor/update domain is usually benign; a raw IP or unknown domain with a tight interval (low **JitterCV**, high **BeaconScore**) is C2-shaped. Confirm the process, then check DNS and the device timeline.

content.json:
## New & cipher-cycling fingerprints
**First-seen**: a JA4 absent from the baseline that just appeared - especially one **spreading to multiple hosts** - is a strong new-implant signal. **Cipher-cycling**: many full-JA4 variants sharing one **JA4_ac** = a tool rotating ciphers to evade hashing (GreyNoise pattern).

**Next step:** prioritize rows with **Spread = spreading (multi-host)** - a brand-new fingerprint appearing on several devices at once can be a deploying implant. Confirm the destination + process and sweep the affected hosts.

content.query:
// Workbook: FIRST-SEEN JA4 in environment (brand-new fingerprints in the recent window).
// A JA4 absent from the whole baseline then appearing in the last `newWindow` is a strong new-implant
// signal - especially if it spreads to multiple hosts. (FoxIO: "a sudden new JA4 in a static estate
// is worth investigating".) Anti-join recent-vs-baseline; no rarity gate (new == inherently rare).
let lookback = {Lookback};
let newWindow = 2d;            // "recent" = last 2 days; everything before is the baseline
let base =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where isnotempty(ja4_)
    | project TimeGenerated, DeviceId, RemoteIP, ja4_, sni;
// Single-pass: compute historic presence AND new-window metrics in ONE scan of `base`
// (was: a materialized distinct + a second scan + leftanti join). SeenHistoric==0 == brand new.
base
| summarize SeenHistoric = countif(TimeGenerated < ago(newWindow)),
            NewConns     = countif(TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow)),
            NewDevices   = dcountif(DeviceId, TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow), 4),
            Dests        = make_set_if(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow), 5),
            FirstSeen    = minif(TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow)),
            LastSeen     = maxif(TimeGenerated, TimeGenerated >= ago(newWindow)) by ja4_
| where SeenHistoric == 0 and NewConns >= 3   // never in baseline (brand new) + not a one-off
| extend Spread = iff(NewDevices >= 2, "spreading (multi-host)", "single host")
| extend cipherCnt = toint(substring(ja4_, 4, 2)), extCnt = toint(substring(ja4_, 6, 2))
| extend Note = strcat(iff(substring(ja4_, 1, 2) in ("10","11","s3","s2"), "legacy-TLS ", ""),
                       iff(substring(ja4_, 3, 1) == "i", "no-SNI ", ""), iff(extCnt == 0, "no-ext ", ""),
                       iff(cipherCnt between (1 .. 3), "few-ciphers ", ""))
| project ja4_, NewDevices, NewConns, Spread, Note, Dests, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| sort by NewDevices desc, NewConns desc

## C2 tradecraft - process vs fingerprint contradictions
**Process -> JA4 mismatch**: the JA4 b-section (chars 11-22) is a stable TLS-library identifier; a browser library from a non-browser process, Python/Go from a LOLBIN, or any TLS from a script-host LOLBIN is high-signal (uTLS parroting / DLL injection / loaders). Microsoft destinations are excluded.

**Next step:** the **Mismatch** column says exactly what is contradictory. Confirm the **Proc** + **ProcFolders** - a known tool in a normal path can be allowlisted; an unknown binary in a user/temp path presenting a browser fingerprint is a strong implant lead - escalate.

let csJa4s = "t120300_c030_52d195ce1d92";
let csCsec = "16bbda4055b2";
let suspProcs = dynamic(["rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","cmd.exe","msiexec.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","msbuild.exe","hh.exe","installutil.exe","regsvcs.exe","regasm.exe"]);
let msSni = dynamic([".microsoft.com",".windows.com",".windowsupdate.com",".office.com",".office365.com",".sharepoint.com",".onedrive.com",".microsoftonline.com",".azure.com",".azureedge.net",".msedge.net",".msftconnecttest.com",".windows.net"]);
let ssl =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), ja4s_ = tostring(AF.ja4s), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where strlen(ja4_) >= 36
    | extend tlsVer = substring(ja4_, 1, 2), alpn = substring(ja4_, 8, 2), csec = substring(ja4_, 24, 12)
    | extend isPublic = (isnotempty(RemoteIP) and ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) == false)
    | where isPublic and ((tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00") or ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
    | summarize Conns = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ja4_, ja4s_, tlsVer, alpn, csec, sni;
let procs =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess" and isnotempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
    | summarize ProcFolder = take_any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, Proc = tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName);
ssl
| join hint.shufflekey=DeviceId kind=inner procs on DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| extend exactCs   = (ja4s_ == csJa4s or csec == csCsec)
| extend shape     = (tlsVer == "12" and alpn == "00")
| extend suspProc  = (Proc in~ (suspProcs))
| extend isUserPath = (ProcFolder has "appdata" or ProcFolder hasprefix "c:\\users\\" or ProcFolder has "\\temp\\")
| extend isMsDest  = (isnotempty(sni) and sni has_any (msSni))
| where exactCs or (shape and suspProc and not(isMsDest))
| extend Why = strcat(iff(csec == csCsec, "CS client c-section; ", ""), iff(ja4s_ == csJa4s, "CS server JA4S; ", ""),
                      iff(shape, "TLS1.2+no-ALPN; ", ""), iff(suspProc, strcat("proc ", Proc, "; "), ""), iff(isUserPath, "user/temp path; ", ""))
| summarize Conns = sum(Conns), Devices = dcount(DeviceId, 4), Procs = make_set(Proc, 5), Dests = make_set(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), 6),
            AnyExactCs = max(exactCs), AnyUserPath = max(isUserPath), Why = take_any(Why), FirstSeen = min(FirstSeen), LastSeen = max(LastSeen)
        by ja4_, ja4s_
| extend Verdict = iff(AnyExactCs, "CRITICAL - Cobalt Strike fingerprint", "HIGH - C2 TLS shape + suspicious process")
| project Verdict, ja4_, ja4s_, Why, Procs, Devices, Conns, AnyUserPath, Dests, FirstSeen, LastSeen
| sort by Verdict asc, Devices asc, Conns asc
| take 200   // display cap: highest-severity first; detection logic above is uncapped

content.json: ### ECH / encrypted-SNI (behavioral review)

content.query:
// ============================================================================
// wb_ech.kql - ECH / ENCRYPTED-SNI FLAG
//              Microsoft Sentinel (Log Analytics)
// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
// ECH (Encrypted Client Hello, RFC 9180) hides the real SNI from network-layer
// inspection by encrypting the inner ClientHello. Two observable JA4 proxies:
//   1. JA4 pos-3 == "i" (no cleartext SNI) AND ALPN in ("h1","h2") to a PUBLIC
//      destination: browser-like traffic with the real destination hidden.
//      This is the most common ECH/ESNI signal in a Defender-inspected estate.
//   2. SNI == "cloudflare-ech.com", endswith "-ech.com", or sni has "ech.":
//      the ECH bootstrap hostname Cloudflare publishes as the outer server name
//      in the initial ClientHello before the encrypted inner hello is delivered.
// ECH is RARE-BUT-RISING: Cloudflare enabled it by default for ~25% of HTTPS in
// 2023; Chrome 117+ uses it automatically for Cloudflare origins. Browser-initiated
// ECH is EXPECTED and labelled INFO. Non-browser or LOLBIN ECH warrants triage
// because implants and proxy tools leverage ECH to blind SNI-based detection.
// All rows are surfaced; triage by Verdict, process, and TLS library.
// Sources: RFC 9180; draft-ietf-tls-esni-24; Cloudflare ECH blog (2023).
// ============================================================================
let lookback = {Lookback};
let browserProcs = dynamic([
    "chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","opera.exe","vivaldi.exe",
    "arc.exe","msedgewebview2.exe","teams.exe","ms-teams.exe","slack.exe","code.exe","discord.exe"]);
let lolbinProcs = dynamic([
    "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe",
    "cscript.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","msbuild.exe","installutil.exe","regasm.exe",
    "regsvcs.exe","cmstp.exe","wmic.exe","hh.exe","cmd.exe","msiexec.exe"]);
// Base: detect ECH proxy signals before the process join.
// strlen >= 10 ensures enough bytes to read the ALPN field at positions 8-9.
let ssl =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "SslConnectionInspected"
    | where AdditionalFields has "ja4"
    | extend AF = todynamic(AdditionalFields)
    | extend ja4_ = tostring(AF.ja4), ja4s_ = tostring(AF.ja4s), sni = tostring(AF.server_name)
    | where isnotempty(ja4_) and strlen(ja4_) >= 10
    | extend isPublic    = (isnotempty(RemoteIP) and ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP) == false)
    | extend noSniFlag   = (substring(ja4_, 3, 1) == "i")
    | extend alpn        = substring(ja4_, 8, 2)
    | extend browserAlpn = (alpn in ("h2","h1"))
    // ECH bootstrap: outer SNI is a known ECH placeholder; real dest is encrypted.
    | extend echBootSni  = (isnotempty(sni)
                            and (sni == "cloudflare-ech.com"
                                 or sni endswith "-ech.com"
                                 or sni has "ech."))
    // ECH candidate: no cleartext SNI in JA4, but ALPN signals a browser-like
    // HTTP session to a public IP — real destination is opaque.
    | extend echNoSni    = (noSniFlag and browserAlpn and isPublic)
    | where isPublic and (echBootSni or echNoSni)
    | extend EchCategory = iff(echBootSni,
                               "ECH-bootstrap-SNI",
                               "no-SNI+browser-ALPN (ECH candidate)")
    | project TimeGenerated, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteIP, RemotePort,
              ja4_, ja4s_, sni, alpn, EchCategory;
// Process attribution: leftouter so ECH from kernel / system paths still surfaces.
// Unattributed rows (no ConnectionSuccess match) are labelled explicitly.
let procs =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback) and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
      and isnotempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
    | summarize Proc       = take_any(tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName)),
                ProcFolder = take_any(InitiatingProcessFolderPath),
                Signer     = take_any(InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName)
            by DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort;
ssl
| join hint.shufflekey=DeviceId kind=leftouter procs on DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| extend Proc          = coalesce(Proc, "(unattributed)")
| extend bsec          = substring(ja4_, 11, 12)
| extend LibHint       = case(
    bsec in ("8daaf6152771","55b375c5d22e"), "Chromium",
    bsec == "5b57614c22b0",                  "Firefox",
    bsec == "85036bcba153",                  "Python",
    bsec in ("9dc949149365","f57a46bbacb6"), "Go",
    bsec == "d83cc789557e",                  "WinINET",
    bsec == "76e208dd3e22",                  "WinHTTP",
    bsec == "fcb5b95cb75a",                  "SoftEther",
    "other")
| extend isBrowserProc = (Proc in~ (browserProcs))
| extend isLolbin      = (Proc in~ (lolbinProcs))
| extend isUserPath    = (ProcFolder has "appdata" or ProcFolder hasprefix "c:\\users\\"
                           or ProcFolder has "\\temp\\" or ProcFolder has "\\downloads\\")
| summarize
    Devices        = dcount(DeviceId, 4),
    Conns          = count(),
    Procs          = make_set(Proc, 6),
    SampleDests    = make_set(coalesce(sni, tostring(RemoteIP)), 6),
    SampleDevices  = make_set(DeviceName, 4),
    AnyBrowserProc = max(isBrowserProc),
    AnyLolbin      = max(isLolbin),
    AnyUserPath    = max(isUserPath),
    LibHint        = take_any(LibHint),
    FirstSeen      = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen       = max(TimeGenerated)
  by ja4_, ja4s_, alpn, EchCategory
| extend Verdict = case(
    AnyLolbin,
        "HIGH - ECH/no-SNI from LOLBIN (triage immediately)",
    not(AnyBrowserProc) and AnyUserPath,
        "MEDIUM - ECH from user-path non-browser process",
    not(AnyBrowserProc),
        "REVIEW - ECH from non-browser process",
        "INFO - ECH from browser (expected; low priority)")
| extend Why = strcat(
    EchCategory, "; ",
    iff(AnyLolbin,
        strcat("LOLBIN [", tostring(Procs), "]; "), ""),
    iff(AnyUserPath and not(AnyBrowserProc),
        "user-path proc; ", ""),
    iff(AnyBrowserProc,
        "browser-initiated (normal); ", "non-browser-initiated; "),
    strcat("TLS-lib=", LibHint, "; ALPN=", alpn))
// Numeric sort key: HIGH=1, MEDIUM=2, REVIEW=3, INFO=4
| extend SevOrd = case(
    AnyLolbin,                           1,
    not(AnyBrowserProc) and AnyUserPath, 2,
    not(AnyBrowserProc),                 3,
    4)
| project Verdict, EchCategory, ja4_, ja4s_, Why, Procs, LibHint,
          SampleDests, SampleDevices, Devices, Conns,
          AnyBrowserProc, AnyLolbin, AnyUserPath, FirstSeen, LastSeen, SevOrd
| sort by SevOrd asc, Devices desc
| project-away SevOrd

## Inventory & hygiene
**Deprecated TLS**: every client still negotiating TLS 1.0/1.1/SSL (PCI 4.0 / FedRAMP). **Impossible JA4**: structurally-impossible fingerprints (legacy-TLS-with-ALPN, zero-extension, scanner prefixes) = synthetic/tooling. **Fleet hygiene**: a device emitting a fingerprint from the wrong OS (cross-compiled / WSL / spoof), or with an abnormal number of distinct JA4s (randomizing implant).

## JA4 rarity landscape
Fingerprints by **combined rarity** (0 = ubiquitous, 1 = singleton on both hosts and connections). The **right-hand bars are the rare tail** - your hunting ground; the **Min rarity** selector focuses every other section there.

**Known-bad JA4+JA4S lookup (opt-in).** This is the **only** component that uses external reference data: FoxIO's **public** ja4plus-mapping (Sliver, IcedID, Cobalt Strike, SoftEther) - a free public reference embedded as a static table, **not** a premium feed. An exact JA4+JA4S pair hit is **CRITICAL** on its own. Set **Known-bad lookup = On** above to run it. Verify hits before acting; fingerprints rotate ~yearly.

## Glossary - what each NUMBER means

Hover any column in a results grid for the same definition. Verdicts/Severity always read: **Critical** (red, act now) > **High** > **Medium** (amber) > **Low / Info** (blue).

- **Score** (Top Leads) - 0-100, the strongest engine score for that lead (suspicion, beaconing or incident-fidelity). Higher = more urgent.
- **SuspicionScore** (triage) - 0-100 from weighted malice signals (LOLBIN +35, legacy-TLS +25, self-signed-to-public +30, no-extensions +20, self-CA +10, SNI=IP +15, few/many ciphers, no-SNI, user-path, no-ALPN) minus benign discounts (aged & widespread -15, known-good library+process -30, Microsoft-issued cert -20). >=80 Critical, >=50 High, >=30 Medium.
- **MatchFidelity** (incident match) - 0-100 = 100 x specificity x temporal-decay x rarity x incident-severity. How tightly the fingerprint ties to an incident. >=75 High, >=40 Medium, >=20 Low.
- **MatchTier** - the closeness of an incident match: flow-exact (same TLS flow) > host+time > host-any > shared-IP.
- **BeaconScore** - 0-100 = 100 x (1 - jitter). 100 = a perfectly periodic call-home (classic C2 shape).
- **JitterCV** - coefficient of variation (stdev/mean) of the gaps between connections. Below ~0.2 = very regular = suspicious; above ~1 = irregular = usually benign.
- **MeanIntervalMin / SpanHours** - average minutes between call-homes / hours the beacon series spanned.
- **Rarity** - 0 to 1 combined inverse-prevalence: a fingerprint is rare only when seen on FEW hosts AND in FEW connections. 1.0 = a near-unique fingerprint. The **Min rarity** selector gates every detection on this.
- **Devices / DistinctDevices** - endpoints that showed the fingerprint (fewer = rarer / more targeted). **Conns / TotalConnections** - connections observed.
- **DistinctJA4s** - number of distinct full fingerprints. Many under one **JA4_ac** = cipher-cycling; many on one device = a randomising implant.
- **DaysActive** - first-seen to last-seen span. Long + widespread = usually legitimate software.
- **TimeGapMin / MinutesFromEmail / ClosestMatch** - minutes between the JA4 callout and the corroborating event (risky sign-in, cloud upload, phishing email, incident alert). Smaller = a stronger correlation.
- **bsec / Client JA4 / Server JA4S** - the **b-section** (JA4 chars 11-22) is the TLS-LIBRARY id; **ja4_** / **ja4s_** are the client / server fingerprints; **ja4_ac** = a+c sections (drops the cipher hash) to track cipher-cycling actors.
- **cipherCnt / extCnt** - number of ciphers / extensions in the ClientHello. Zero extensions or fewer than 4 ciphers = a primitive/hand-rolled tool; more than ~60 ciphers = a scanner or SoftEther.

## Glossary - what each QUERY (section) finds

- **Top Leads** - the single ranked queue across all engines, plus process-contradictions and Cobalt Strike shapes. *Start here.*
- **Suspicious triage** - every rare JA4 x JA4S pair scored for maliciousness (process + certificate + structure signals).
- **Incident match** - rare fingerprints tied to your existing incidents, ranked by closeness.
- **Beaconing** - regular, low-jitter TLS call-homes (the classic C2 shape).
- **New & cipher-cycling** - brand-new fingerprints in the estate, and tools rotating ciphers to evade hashing (JA4_ac).
- **C2 tradecraft** - a TLS library that does not match its process (uTLS parroting / injection / PUP), and TLS 1.2 + no-ALPN Cobalt Strike / Meterpreter shapes.
- **Endpoint corroboration** - a rare JA4 fused with a Mark-of-the-Web download (dropper), an ASR/Defender alert on the same device (detonation), or suspicious process lineage.
- **Identity & cloud** - a rare JA4 fused with a risky Entra sign-in (AiTM session theft), an unsanctioned-app cloud upload (exfil), or a delivered phishing email (phish -> implant).
- **Destination anomalies** - rare JA4 to a TRUSTED cloud/CDN from a non-browser (living-off-trusted-sites), encrypted/absent SNI (ECH), and non-browser libraries reaching external SaaS (shadow-IT).
- **Inventory & hygiene** - clients still using deprecated TLS (compliance), structurally-impossible/synthetic fingerprints, and cross-OS / fingerprint-proliferation anomalies.
- **Process baseline** *(reference)* - the known-good (process -> TLS library) catalog, used to learn the estate and tune the mismatch allowlists.
- **Rarity landscape** *(reference)* - the distribution of fingerprints by rarity; the right-hand bars are the rare tail you hunt in.
- **Known-bad (opt-in)** - exact matches against FoxIO's public known-malware JA4+JA4S list (toggle on).

### Methodology & sources
MISP decaying models (temporal decay) | Pyramid of Pain (JA4 ranks above IP) | JA4 / JA4+ spec (structure decode, JA4_ac) | FoxIO ja4plus-mapping (opt-in known-bad only) | detection-engineering proximity scoring | Recorded Future / STIX confidence bands. Core signals are Microsoft E5 / Defender-native - no premium external feeds.
